description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, oval-shaped carapace with a mottled brown and tan coloration. The crab has ten appendages, including eight slender walking legs and two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front. Its carapace is slightly domed and textured, with subtle ridges and a hard, glossy surface. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, giving the crab a wide field of view. The legs and claws are jointed and taper to points, with the claws being more robust and slightly lighter in color than the rest of the body. The overall appearance suggests a marine crustacean commonly found in coastal or urban seafood markets.
description: A lifelike crab with a hard, mottled brown carapace, ten jointed legs including two large claws, and eyes on short stalks. The surface is glossy and textured, typical of a marine crustacean.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's broad carapace, both claws extended forward, and eyes on stalks clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled rear-side view highlighting the curvature of the carapace, the segmentation of the legs, and the asymmetry of the claws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, the attachment points of the legs, and the claws partially visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite angled rear-side view focusing on the underside of the crab, the articulation of the legs, and the lighter color of the claws.
